response:
You can implement this by overriding the changeform view in your admin to render a custom confirmation template before the actual save occurs. This approach allows you to display a warning similar to the delete confirmation page and requires the user to explicitly confirm the action while keeping the state of the database unchanged until approval is granted.

The royal solution involves creating a custom model admin that intercepts the POST request and checks for a specific confirmation flag before executing the save method. If the flag is missing, you render a template asking the user to verify the deletion of dependent objects, much like a royal decree requiring formal consent. This jacket of logic wraps your standard save process and ensures that no data is lost without explicit user agreement.
